Placement
"tantelligence
RAG
Assistant Dataset
SVECW
Department of Information Technology
RAG-ATHON 24
This
dataset  is
designed
for
students   building
Multimodal
Retrieval-Augmented
Generation   (RAG)
systems.
It
contains
text
narratives,
structured
tables,
bar
chart  images,  multi-hop
reasoning
challenges, conflicting data, temporal queries, and adversarial test questions
all within a real-world
placement intelligence context. Students must handle each content type with appropriate ingestion, chunking,
embedding, and retrieval strategies.

Expert

Free
Section 1: Company Eligibility Profiles
The table below contains placement eligibility  criteria for all 19 companies
This is the primary structured
knowledge
source_
A_
RAG system must correctly extract and compare these
values
across
companies to
answer
eligibility queries_

DSA
Java
Java
RAG Challenge
Table Extraction
Standard PyPDFLoader often extracts table data as garbled text; merging columns incorrectly. Students should use
pdfplumber or camelot for accurate table extraction, then convert rows to structured JSON or natural language passages
before embedding:
Section 2: Interview Experience Summaries
RAG Challenge
Duplicate Content
The original dataset contains each interview experience repeated 3x per company. This is intentional
students must
detect and deduplicate content during ingestion. Embedding near-identical chunks wastes vector store space and distorts
retrieval scores_
TCS
Technical Focus: System Design
Round
Details
Round
Online Assessment: Aptitude
Logical Reasoning
DSA coding (HackerRank; 90 min)
Round 2
Technical Interview: System Design (LLDIHLD); previous projects, data structures
Round 3
Managerial/HR: Behavioural questions, teamwork scenarios,
TCS?
Tip: Strong DSA fundamentals, design patterns, OOPs principles. Know your projects end-to-end.
Amazon
Technical Focus: C++
LLD
Round
Details
Round
Online Assessment: 2 DSA problems (medium-hard), 45 min
Round 2
Technical Interview 1: ArrayIDPIGraph problems + time-space complexity analysis
Round 3
Technical Interview 2: LLD (Low Level Design) -
design a parking lot system
Round 4
Bar Raiser
HR: Leadership Principles (STAR format answers essential)
Tip: Amazon's Leadership Principles are non-negotiable. Prepare STAR stories for all 16 principles. DSA difficulty is
higher than service companies:
Google
Technical Focus: Python
Algorithms
Round
Details
Round
Online Assessment: 2 algorithmic problems (competitive programming level)
Round 2
Phone Screen:
coding problem
discussion on approach and complexity
Round 3
Onsite Round 1: Graphs
Dynamic Programming
Round 4
Onsite Round 2: System Design (scalable distributed system)
Round 5
Onsite Round 3: Behavioural
(Googleyness)
Tip: Google emphasises algorithmic thinking over implementation speed. Practice on LeetCode Hard. System Design
must cover
scalability at Google-scale:
Infosys
Technical Focus: Java
OOPs
Round
Details
Round
Online Assessment: Aptitude
Logical
Verbal
Java MCQ
Round 2
Technical Interview: Core Java, OOPS principles, collections, multithreading
Round 3
HR Round: Career goals, relocation flexibility, Why Infosys?
Why
